name: FastDomainCheck MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for fast domain name availability checking and WHOIS lookups. Enables AI agents to check if domain names are available for registration, retrieve WHOIS information, query DNS records, and support domain research workflows — useful for brand naming, product launches, and domain portfolio management.
use_cases: Checking domain availability in bulk from business naming agents
Retrieving WHOIS information for domain research from analysis agents
Querying DNS records for domain investigations from DevOps agents
Supporting brand and product naming workflows with AI-assisted domain research
not_for: Real-time production DNS monitoring (use dedicated DNS tools)
Authoritative DNS management (use Route53 or Cloudflare MCPs)
best_when: A product team or entrepreneur wants AI agents to check domain availability and retrieve WHOIS data during naming and brand research — automating the bulk domain checking workflow.
avoid_when: You need to register domains or manage DNS records — this is read-only availability checking. Use registrar APIs for registration.

auth.notes: No authentication required for WHOIS queries and domain availability checks via public services.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — uses public WHOIS and DNS services
pricing.notes: Free open source. Queries public WHOIS servers and DNS — no paid API required.

performance.rate_limits: WHOIS servers impose rate limits — bulk checking may hit throttling

known_agent_gotchas: WHOIS servers rate limit aggressive querying — implement delays between bulk checks
WHOIS data accuracy varies by registrar and TLD — not guaranteed real-time
Some TLDs (newer gTLDs) may not have reliable WHOIS data via public servers
Domain availability can change between check and registration — race conditions possible
agent_readiness.security_notes: Read-only domain/WHOIS queries. No credentials stored. Public DNS/WHOIS data only. Low security risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: Read-only public data queries. No credentials. Low security risk.
